claim: Known findings only; undiscovered defects remain possible.

findings.exploit: Consumers can resolve unpublished internal distributions or receive a wheel missing import packages.
findings.correction: One root distribution contains all five import packages and has no unpublished cpcf-* dependencies.

findings.exploit: A first-time agent requires an API before it can discover CPCF semantics or schemas.
findings.correction: Offline explain, self-check, schema list/show, and bundle verification are installed in the base wheel.

findings.exploit: Local paths, credentials, caches, or generated artifacts can be committed or packaged.
findings.correction: Explicit source manifest, staged/archive hygiene scanner, narrow Gitleaks allowlist, and expanded ignores.

findings.exploit: A manual workflow or mismatched tag can upload an unreviewed distribution.
findings.correction: Release-only trusted-publishing job, exact artifact contract, version match, protected pypi environment, explicit kadubon self-review governance downgrade, and disabled publication variable.

findings.exploit: Previously admitted records can be used without recomputing signatures, time, source, lifecycle, and projection authority.
findings.correction: A single loader now recomputes the ledger, DSSE, trusted time, lifecycle, source artifacts, evidence pointers, and quorum while rejecting unsigned native authority; API-wide integration remains required.
findings.regression_test: tests/test_v6_authority.py and release gate: authoritative read integration suite

findings.exploit: A crash can separate CAS references, ledger state, idempotency, outbox, audit, and current generation.
findings.correction: The PostgreSQL generation unit of work now groups ledger, generation, audit, idempotency, outbox, quarantine, and pointer changes under serializable optimistic locking; live crash-matrix evidence and API-wide use remain required.
findings.regression_test: tests/test_v6_db_worker_assurance.py::test_generation_unit_of_work_commits_all_database_effects_or_quarantines and release gate: PostgreSQL generation crash matrix

findings.exploit: A stale or replayed runner lease can submit unbound output or hide incomplete cleanup.
findings.correction: The reference gateway now validates sanitized certificate identities, independently signed capability and execution policies, leases, attempts, bounded artifacts, DSSE receipts, cleanup, and pending-only projections. A multi-replica PostgreSQL repository, deployed Envoy sidecar, and integration evidence remain required.
findings.regression_test: tests/test_v6_runner_gateway.py and release gate: deployed mTLS lease replay and artifact integrity suite

findings.exploit: Incomplete siphon, coupling, cut, service-curve, or occurrence analysis can be mistaken for a negative result.
findings.correction: Closed result kinds and bounded exact reference algorithms now preserve exhaustion; large-network and mutation/differential release evidence remains required.

findings.exploit: A perturbation can reuse baseline facts after expiry, revocation, or a typed value change.
findings.correction: Object-removal replay exists; complete reduced-snapshot transformation classes remain required.
findings.regression_test: release gate: fresh-audit equivalence for every perturbation class

findings.exploit: An abstract planner successor can retain science, authority, trial, or independence that an outcome removed.
findings.correction: Every branch must invoke the shared audit kernel over a complete successor snapshot.
findings.regression_test: release gate: full-snapshot AND-OR differential suite

findings.exploit: Unsigned coordination or trial records can be treated as admitted upstream state.
findings.correction: Authoritative admission must recompute every event and trial quorum.
findings.regression_test: release gate: coordination and trial quorum admission suite

findings.exploit: Onboarding can present static blockers that omit or misstate live workspace state.
findings.correction: Aggregate every trust, time, ledger, science, planner, runner, projection, coordination, trial, quota, and repair subsystem.
findings.regression_test: release gate: installed-wheel live onboarding conformance

findings.exploit: Passing regression counts can conceal weak branch or mutation assurance.
findings.correction: 95 percent critical branch, 90 percent overall, 85 percent mutation, integration, load, chaos, restore, soak, threat-model, and penetration gates remain blocking.

findings.exploit: The v0.6 read-only legacy bridge can forward a v0.1-v0.5 mutation or local process command.
findings.correction: The bridge uses an explicit read-only command registry and rejects apply or mutation commands before legacy dispatch.

findings.exploit: Platform newline conversion, OS-specific type surfaces, invalid PostgreSQL utility binding, or an incomplete Mutmut shadow tree can make release evidence non-portable or absent.
findings.correction: Generated references force LF, platform-only APIs use runtime module dictionaries, role DDL uses a safely composed literal, and Mutmut copies the full source tree while mutating only critical modules.

findings.exploit: An online Alembic command can report a successful upgrade while its uncommitted DDL is rolled back when the connection closes.
findings.correction: The online migration now configures and executes every revision inside Alembic's explicit transaction boundary.

findings.exploit: A Mutmut results command can fail before computing the release score, leaving mutation assurance absent despite a completed mutation run.
findings.correction: CI and release workflows pass an explicit Boolean value to Mutmut 3.6 and retain the 85 percent fail-closed score check.

findings.exploit: A repository owner can approve the pypi environment and bypass independent human release review.
findings.correction: The pypi environment records kadubon with prevent_self_review=false exactly as configured, and all audit and release documents label this as self-approval rather than independent review.
findings.regression_test: GitHub environment settings audit and docs/release.md
